sectionsUnavailable: Generated Sequencing.com AI Reports output, service provenance, model prompt, model version, calibrated disease-screening output, disease or trait interpretation, and model quality evidence are unavailable for the local fixture.
Public AI Reports Skin Health sample rows are included only as observed output-structure evidence and are not local personal results.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A selected public BDNF rs6265 identifier is available for lookup and annotation-context validation, but no local trait, disease, medication, clinical, or AI-generated interpretation is supplied.
resultRows.sourceLabel: dbSNP / Ensembl / Sequence Ontology reference context
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This row demonstrates the local utility boundary: public identifiers can anchor lookup context, but the local run must not infer disease risk, traits, diagnosis, treatment, or model accuracy.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The fixture supplies derived GRCh38 coordinate context while explicitly omitting alternate-build conversion, remapping evidence, service output, and generated AI report provenance.
resultRows.sourceLabel: Ensembl REST / NCBI Variation Services reference context
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Reference-build context can be reported, but it does not prove that a Sequencing.com AI report was generated or that any disease-screening conclusion is available.

resultRows.item: AI Reports service output and provenance
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Generated AI report output, model prompt, model version, service provenance, local disease or trait interpretation, and calibrated quality evidence are unavailable in this fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The local report can explain missing evidence and safety limits, but it must not create a new Sequencing.com-style AI diagnosis, disease-risk result, medication result, treatment plan, or all-clear statement.

resultRows.item: AI Reports Skin Health beta sample
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The cover identifies AI Reports as a Skin Health beta sample report for Genome1 using WGS data.
resultRows.description: This row captures sample-report identity and input-label structure only; it does not show that a local AI report was generated.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 1 of the public sample PDF; keep cover metadata separate from local generated-report provenance.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The summary page reports 173 conditions screened, 212 genes screened, and 7,579 genetic variants screened.
resultRows.description: These are sample-report summary counts and must not be treated as local genome screening coverage without supplied service output.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public sample PDF; counts are observed sample output, not local panel coverage.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report shows a red-category KRT14 finding for Epidermolysis Bullosa Simplex with variant identifiers rs58645163 and RCV000487370, sample data TT, and risk status Likely Detected.
resultRows.description: This is a public sample-person row only; local output must not reuse TT or Likely Detected as a personal disease, risk, pathogenicity, or diagnosis result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 4 of the public sample PDF; the KRT14 row is exact sample output, not a local finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The red-category detail continues with condition description, symptom examples, diagnostic criteria, treatment options, professional care language, and next-step headings.
resultRows.description: These headings define the official output shape; local output must not convert them into medical advice or treatment recommendations.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 4-5 of the public sample PDF; medical-action headings are observed structure only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The Additional Findings, Carrier Status for Inherited Conditions, and Medications That May Be Impacted By Your Genes sections each state that no genetic conditions were found in that section under high-confidence results.
resultRows.description: The empty-category language is sample output only and must not be used as local all-clear reassurance, carrier-status absence, or medication-response absence.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 6-8 of the public sample PDF; do not infer local orange, yellow, teal, carrier, or medication status.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The No Increased Genetic Risk section reports 7,570 total conditions screened, and the Unknown Genetic Risk section reports 8 total conditions screened.
resultRows.description: These count-only sections are observed sample structure and must not be reused as local no-increased-risk or unknown-risk conclusions.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 9-10 of the public sample PDF; count rows are not local screening results.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The back matter includes a multi-page list of analyzed conditions, glossary terms including KRT14 and keratin, and references to ClinVar, Genetic Home Reference, NIH, and ClinicalTrials.gov.
resultRows.description: These pages define the sample report's appendix style and public reference labels; local output should cite supplied references without inventing condition coverage.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from pages 11-17 of the public sample PDF; condition-list coverage and reference labels are sample output only.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The disclaimer states the report is generated by the AI Reports beta feature powered by SequencingAI, is not FDA evaluated, may contain incorrect AI-generated information, depends on source data quality, and is not medical advice.
resultRows.description: The disclaimer language is mandatory safety context for this sample-backed package and does not validate local model accuracy.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 18 of the public sample PDF; preserve beta, AI, FDA, data-quality, and medical-advice limitations.

utilityContext.plainEnglishMeaning: This package is a local, reproducible AI Reports boundary scaffold. It can show supplied utility context and observed sample-report structure, but cannot replace Sequencing.com service output or qualified medical review.
unsupportedClaims: No claim that a Sequencing.com AI report was generated for the local fixture.
No local EBS, KRT14, TT, Likely Detected, disease-risk, carrier-status, medication-response, pathogenicity, diagnosis, treatment, screening, or actionability claim.
No model-accuracy, FDA-review, data-quality, or panel-coverage claim beyond supplied evidence.

references.title: AI Reports Skin Health beta sample report

genotypeSummary.effect: The public sample report labels this KRT14 row Likely Detected for Epidermolysis Bullosa Simplex.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local EBS, disease risk, pathogenicity, diagnosis, treatment, or screening conclusions.

genotypeSummary.effect: The public sample report states 173 conditions, 212 genes, and 7,579 variants were screened in the sample output.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample count context only; do not claim local panel coverage, no-increased-risk status, unknown-risk status, or all-clear reassurance.

uncertainty: No calibrated AI Reports model output, disease-screening output, model version, or service provenance is supplied.
The public sample PDF is a sample-person Skin Health beta report and is not the local user genome result.
missingInputs: clinical review and confirmatory testing context
limitations: Do not emit raw genome data, raw SNP tables, sequencing files, alignment files, or full callsets.
Public sample KRT14/EBS rows are observed output structure only and must not be treated as local findings.
Do not recommend medication, screening, diagnostic testing, reproductive decisions, lifestyle changes, diet, supplements, treatment changes, or professional-care actions from this fixture.
Consult qualified healthcare professionals for medical questions; this result is not medical advice.
